title: Quantum Sensor Noise Study
description: Model a simple sensing problem and compare how signal quality changes as coherence, noise, and readout limitations shift.
whyItMatters: Quantum sensing only matters if the measurement chain beats the practical noise budget. This project builds the habit of evaluating claimed precision in system terms.

steps.title: Define the sensing problem
steps.description: Choose a measurable quantity such as magnetic field, frequency drift, or phase shift.
steps.tips: Simple models are enough if the signal path is clear.

steps.title: Model the ideal response
steps.description: Set a best-case signal curve before adding noise sources.
steps.tips: You need a clean reference to see what noise is destroying.

steps.title: Add practical noise terms
steps.description: Introduce coherence limits, readout uncertainty, or loss and compare the resulting sensitivity.
steps.tips: One or two honest noise sources teach more than a huge unrealistic model.

steps.title: Run a parameter sweep
steps.description: Change one key parameter and show when the sensing advantage becomes credible or disappears.
steps.tips: This is where the system-level trade-off becomes visible.

steps.title: Summarise the engineering implication
steps.description: Explain what would have to improve for the sensor concept to matter in practice.
steps.tips: Make the conclusion operational, not only mathematical.

deliverables: Short engineering conclusion on what limits the concept

rubric.description: The signal and noise assumptions are understandable and internally consistent.
The analysis reveals when the sensing concept is or is not meaningful.
The conclusion connects the model to real engineering priorities.
